email_summary: Strauss ordered 65,000 units of ESP-CHOC-350 for Q1 2026, which was dispatched February 2 and delivered to the Holon facility without issue. For Q2, Strauss confirmed 70,000 units, but EuroPack unilaterally proposed a spec change to 380gsm board at PLN 1.93/unit and began scheduling Q2 production on the new spec before receiving Strauss approval. Strauss explicitly rejected the unapproved spec change on March 12 and March 20, and on April 8 directed EuroPack to proceed on the original ESP-CHOC-350 spec at PLN 1.85/unit, pushing delivery to late April. The 380gsm upgrade and its associated price increase remain unresolved for Q3, and no formal change order has been executed.

contract_terms.key_penalty: Specification non-conformance: supplier bears full cost of replacement shipment meeting the contracted spec, and Strauss is not obligated to accept or pay for non-conforming goods.

open_issues.description: Contract STR-PACK-2025-005 expired on 2026-03-31 and no signed renewal or replacement contract has been referenced in any email or contract file. Q2 is being executed without a current active contract.
open_issues.source_ref: Contract STR-PACK-2025-005, Effective Date / Term fields (2025-04-01, 12 months)

open_issues.description: EuroPack proposed a unilateral switch from contracted SKU ESP-CHOC-350 (350gsm) to ESP-CHOC-380 (380gsm, EP-COR-380-B) mid-contract; no written change order signed by both parties exists, as required by the contract. Strauss has rejected the change for Q2 but left the door open for Q3 if a proper change order is prepared.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-02-17 from Marek Wojcik; email 2026-03-20 from David Cohen; contract STR-PACK-2025-005 Pricing clause (change order requirement)

open_issues.description: EuroPack issued a pro-forma invoice at PLN 1.93/unit for ESP-CHOC-380, which is PLN 0.08/unit (approx. 4.3%) above the contracted price of PLN 1.85/unit for ESP-CHOC-350, without an executed written change order. The Q2 order has been reverted to PLN 1.85/unit, but the pricing for any future spec change remains unresolved.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-03-18 from Katarzyna Wiśniewska (pro-forma invoice); email 2026-04-08 from David Cohen (reversion to PLN 1.85/unit); contract STR-PACK-2025-005 Pricing clause

open_issues.description: EuroPack's internal coordination failure — Agnieszka was not copied on David's March 12 hold instruction sent to Marek — caused the Q2 run to be incorrectly scheduled on the 380gsm line, consuming the April 6 production slot and pushing Q2 delivery from early April to approximately April 25-28.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-03-25 from Marek Wojcik (acknowledging oversight); email 2026-04-08 from David Cohen (confirming late April delivery accepted)

commodity_benchmark: No benchmark in scope for this category
heads_up: Contract STR-PACK-2025-005 expired on 2026-03-31 and Q2 is being executed with no active signed agreement in place — Strauss should not allow Q3 discussions to proceed without first executing a new contract that explicitly addresses whether the 380gsm spec and PLN 1.93/unit price will be adopted, using the formal written change order process required under the expired contract's pricing clause.
negotiation_brief: The supplier relationship is functional but has a notable coordination failure on record: EuroPack's internal teams proceeded to schedule and invoice for an unapproved spec and price change, requiring multiple escalations from David Cohen before the error was corrected. The contract expired on 2026-03-31, meaning Q2 is running without a current agreement and Q3 has no contractual basis at all — this significantly weakens EuroPack's position and gives Strauss leverage to set the terms of any renewal. On price, EuroPack is pushing for PLN 1.93/unit (a 4.3% increase) tied to the 380gsm upgrade; the technical case is documented (ECT up from 36 to 42 N/mm, stacking strength +18%) and Strauss has acknowledged it is reasonable in principle, but no price should be agreed without a formal change order and a new contract. Walk in ready to negotiate the renewal contract first, then address the 380gsm spec change as a structured amendment within that new agreement — do not allow the spec and price discussions to proceed informally again.

financial_signals.delivery_reliability_score: Good — Q1 shipment dispatched February 2 as scheduled and delivered to Holon on time; Q2 delivery delayed to late April due to internal EuroPack coordination error on spec change, not a logistics failure.

raw_emails.subject: Q1 packaging order — EuroPack
raw_emails.body:
Marek,

Happy new year. We'd like to place Q1: 65,000 units ESP-CHOC-350, standard spec as per contract. Please confirm availability and send a delivery schedule.

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q1 packaging order — EuroPack
raw_emails.body:
David,

Happy new year! Confirmed — 65,000 units ESP-CHOC-350 available for Q1. Production slot is week of January 26, delivery to your site around February 3-5.

I am copying Agnieszka Kowalska, our production coordinator, who will track the order through manufacturing.

Best,
Marek

raw_emails.subject: Production schedule — Q1 Strauss order
raw_emails.body:
Hi David,

This is Agnieszka from production. I have your Q1 order (65,000 units ESP-CHOC-350) scheduled for print run January 26-28, packing January 29-30, dispatch February 2.

Please send your delivery address and preferred unloading hours.

Agnieszka

raw_emails.subject: RE: Production schedule — delivery details
raw_emails.body:
Agnieszka,

Delivery address: Strauss Group, Holon facility, Hamivtza 2, Holon. Receiving hours: 07:00-15:00 Sun-Thu. Contact on arrival: Itzik Peretz, +972-54-1234567.

David

raw_emails.subject: Q1 shipment dispatched
raw_emails.body:
David,

Q1 order of 65,000 units (ESP-CHOC-350) dispatched February 2 via DHL Freight, AWB PL-202602-3841. ETA at Holon facility February 5.

Agnieszka

raw_emails.subject: Q2 planning + important update on board spec
raw_emails.body:
David,

Hope the Q1 delivery was received well. Looking ahead to Q2 — we can support up to 80,000 units if your demand is growing.

The main point of this email: our materials team has reviewed the current ESP-CHOC-350 specification and has recommended an upgrade to 380gsm for structural integrity reasons. Two clients have reported compression issues with 350gsm in hot weather transit. Our engineering team believes 380gsm eliminates this risk entirely. The 380gsm board fits the same die line and print spec — no changes to your artwork or packaging line.

Price impact: PLN 1.93/unit vs PLN 1.85/unit under the current contract — roughly 4.3% increase.

Note: for any custom print runs below 50,000 units, a setup surcharge of PLN 2,800 applies for press setup and colour registration. Your standard volumes are above this threshold.

I have copied Piotr Nowak (technical lead) and Katarzyna Wiśniewska (account management) for any follow-up.

Marek

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 planning + board spec
raw_emails.body:
Marek,

Q2 volume — let's go with 70,000 units. On the board spec change — I've noted your reasoning. I'll need to run this by our packaging team before we can agree to anything. The spec and the pricing are both contract changes and require formal sign-off.

Can you send the technical data sheet for the 380gsm board so our packaging engineers can review?

David

raw_emails.subject: 380gsm technical datasheet
raw_emails.body:
Hi David,

This is Piotr from our technical team. Attached is the datasheet for the 380gsm corrugated board (EuroPack spec EP-COR-380-B). Key improvements over 350gsm:
- Edge crush test (ECT): 42 N/mm vs 36 N/mm on current spec
- Stacking strength: +18%
- Moisture resistance: marginal improvement

All external dimensions and print registration remain identical.

Piotr Nowak
Technical Manager, EuroPack Solutions

raw_emails.subject: Q2 production planning — confirming 380gsm
raw_emails.body:
Hi David,

I am starting to plan the Q2 production run for 70,000 units. Based on Marek's update I have scheduled this on the 380gsm board line (EP-COR-380-B) with start date April 6.

Please confirm delivery address is the same as Q1 — Holon facility.

Agnieszka

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 production planning — HOLD on 380gsm
raw_emails.body:
Agnieszka, Marek,

Delivery address is the same. On the 380gsm — I have not formally confirmed the spec change. Our packaging team is still reviewing. Please do not assume this is agreed. I will confirm separately on the spec question.

Do NOT start a 380gsm production run without my explicit written approval.

David

raw_emails.subject: Q2 order confirmation and invoice preview
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

Thank you for confirming Q2 volume of 70,000 units and delivery address. I have prepared the Q2 pro-forma invoice:

- 70,000 units ESP-CHOC-380 @ PLN 1.93/unit
- Total: PLN 135,100
- Delivery: approx. April 10-12

Please let me know if any changes are needed.

Best regards,
Katarzyna Wiśniewska
Account Manager, EuroPack Solutions

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 order — HOLD on spec — this has not been agreed
raw_emails.body:
Katarzyna,

Do not process this as ESP-CHOC-380. The spec change has not been agreed. Our contract is for ESP-CHOC-350 at PLN 1.85/unit and any change requires a formal written change order signed by both parties.

I told Agnieszka explicitly on March 12 not to start the 380gsm run. Marek, can you confirm with your team that this has been escalated correctly? I am concerned there is a coordination gap.

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 — production hold
raw_emails.body:
David,

Understood — I apologise for the confusion. We have put the Q2 run on hold pending your sign-off. I have spoken with Agnieszka. It appears she was not copied on your March 12 email to me, which is why the 380gsm was still in the schedule. My oversight.

If we miss the April 6 production slot, the next available window is April 20, which pushes delivery to April 25-28. We would appreciate a decision by March 30 to avoid further delay.

Marek

raw_emails.subject: Q2 — proceed on original spec
raw_emails.body:
Marek,

After internal review, we are not in a position to accept the spec change to 380gsm without a formal change order and pricing agreement. Please proceed with Q2 on the original ESP-CHOC-350 spec at PLN 1.85/unit. I understand this pushes delivery to late April.

We can discuss the 380gsm upgrade for Q3 if a proper change order can be prepared and agreed in advance — the technical case is reasonable, we just cannot accept a unilateral spec and price change without paperwork.

David

raw_contracts.supplier_08_europack.md:
# Contract Summary — EuroPack Solutions

**Contract ID:** STR-PACK-2025-005
**Supplier:** EuroPack Solutions (Poland)
**Category:** Packaging — corrugated cardboard boxes (SKU: ESP-CHOC-350, 350gsm board, custom print, Strauss chocolate line)
**Effective Date:** 2025-04-01
**Term:** 12 months
**Renewal Date:** 2026-03-31

## Payment Terms
Net 30 from delivery date.

## Volume Commitment
Minimum 50,000 units/quarter, maximum 120,000 units/quarter.

## Pricing
PLN 1.85/unit (approximately €0.43/unit at contract signing), fixed for the contract term. Any specification change to the contracted SKU requires a written change order signed by authorized representatives of both parties before taking effect; email or verbal agreement does not constitute a valid change order under this contract.

## Penalty Clauses
- Late delivery beyond 3 calendar days: 1% of shipment value per day delayed, capped at 8% of total shipment value.
- Specification non-conformance (goods delivered do not match contracted SKU): supplier bears full cost of replacement shipment meeting the contracted specification; Strauss is not obligated to accept or pay for non-conforming goods.

## Notes
The contracted SKU is ESP-CHOC-350 (350gsm corrugated board). The email thread shows EuroPack's sales contact proposing a unilateral switch to 380gsm board (a different SKU, EP-COR-380-B) mid-contract, citing structural integrity concerns. This proposal was introduced informally by email and was never formally acknowledged or countersigned by Strauss in the form of a written change order as required by this contract. EuroPack's production coordinator subsequently scheduled Q2 production on the 380gsm line before receiving Strauss sign-off. Strauss pushed back and the Q2 order was ultimately reverted to the original 350gsm spec. The prototype should surface this spec-change conflict explicitly: flag the gap between the contracted SKU (ESP-CHOC-350, 350gsm) and the supplier's proposed substitution, confirm no change order exists, and note the incident for the next contract negotiation meeting.
